messages.message: I'm considering moving into Tuscany this turn. Would you support my move if I stay clear of your interests in Burgundy or Marseille?

Hey Italy! You know what they say about Italy/austria: if one of us dies, the other gets screwed over real quick. I'd love to find a way to help one another out this game. If you have any thoughts on openings or strategies, please let me know!
I've noticed your fleet in Trieste is vulnerable to a coordinated move from Venice. Perhaps we can discuss mutual interests in the Adriatic?
Hi France! You up for demilitarizing Burgundy?

Hi there, Germany! I was just curious as to your intentions this game? I'd love to have a good relationship with you, and I'd be more than willing to share my intel if you'd do the same for me. Please let me know if there is any way I can be of assistance!
Hey Russia! Would you be interested in running a Juggernaut? I reckon we could really help each other out throughout the game if we worked together.
Greetings Austria! I'm happy to keep communications open between us. I'd really appreciate a demilitarized Tyrolia, if that works for you. Good luck!
Hi England! I'm really hoping to work with you this game! Let me know how I can help you in the fall, and how we should divide the lowlands.

Hey Turkey! Yes I would definitely be interested in something like that. I reckon it could go a long way, it's such a great alliance, and it's not immediately obvious to the rest of the board so we should be able to disguise it pretty well for a long time, at least in the opening years.
Shall we bounce in the Black Sea?

Hi Italy! Do you have any interest in opening to Piedmont? I'm happy to convince France to join you in a triple alliance if you think that would be a good idea. I think it would be really great to start this game off with you and me and England and France all on good terms. Let me know what you think.
I'm planning to expand along the Italian coast—I'd be interested in understanding your plans for the Aegean and Ionian Seas.

Hello England, I hope we can have a good game.

How would you like to divide up Scandinavia?

You're absolutely right—our positions are tightly linked, especially with the Adriatic being a key region. I'm looking at potential moves into Tuscany or even supporting a shift toward Trieste, depending on how things unfold. If we coordinate, we could keep others from gaining too much leverage in the region. Let me know where your priorities lie.

A triple alliance sounds tempting, especially with France and England. However, I’m cautious about overcommitting too early. My focus is on securing my southern flank and ensuring I’m not squeezed by France or Turkey. I’d be interested in hearing more about your plans for Piedmont and how we could support each other there.

Let's go after Turkey. They don't have the capability of supporting a move into Trieste, so we can stay out of Venice, and I can move into the Adriatic from Trieste. You could move your fleet to Apulia and then convoy that army to Greece in the fall. From there we can put a lot of pressure on him

Of course!

How would you feel about a non-aggression pact at least, in which neither of us will build fleets in Mars and you leave Pie/gulf of Lyon empty, with any fleet building in the more southern waters like Rom/nap?
Hi Russia! Are you up for a demilitarized Baltic, Prussia, and Silesia?

Your idea about moving into the Adriatic and targeting Turkey is intriguing. I'm certainly open to coordinating, but I need to ensure my southern flank remains secure. If you focus on the Adriatic, I could position my fleet in Apulia to support a later push toward Greece. Let me assess the timing and logistics—this could be mutually beneficial.
Hi Russia! How are you? I'm very much hoping we can maintain a friendship this game, and I was wondering what your intentions were for the Balkans and the German/english front?

I appreciate your offer for a non-aggression pact. A mutual agreement on fleet restraint in the north makes strategic sense for both of us. I’m willing to leave Piedmont and the Gulf of Lyon open if you agree not to expand fleets into Burgundy or Marseille. That way, we can focus on securing our own regions without unnecessary friction.

Sure!

How are your plans with England? I would appreciate an early Sealion if you're up for it.
Alright, sounds good. I'm willing to compromise, and I'm always down for mutually beneficial situations

Hi Russia! Good luck this game! I think if you opened with an army in Stp, you could count on my support into Swe next year. If you went with a fleet, then I would have no reason to think that we wouldn't be natural enemies. Sound good?
I plan on moving into the Aegean but that's only as a defensive measure, I'm sure we could work something out to make sure we don't bounce in the Aegean?
OK, let's just keep our options open and we can discuss more after the first year. Good luck!
Hi England! How are you? I'd just like to open up a line of communication with you and wish you the best of luck in your conquest!

That’s a great attitude—mutual benefit is always preferable to conflict. Given our shared interests in the Adriatic, I suggest we keep communication open about our moves. If you're moving toward Trieste, I’ll ensure my army in Venice is positioned to avoid bounces and possibly offer support where it suits us both.
Hi France! How are you? I'd just like to open up a line of communication with you and wish you the best of luck in your conquest!
Yeah that sounds good. Let's bounce in the Black Sea this turn and go from there?
I have nothing from England yet.
In the interest of transparency, I told Italy that I'm moving into the Aegean and Ionian Sea, but I'm just trying to set myself up to attack him.

Greetings!

Best of luck to you as well!

Your focus on the Aegean as a defensive measure aligns with my interest in securing southern Italy without unnecessary confrontation. Let’s coordinate our movements carefully so we avoid clashing in the Ionian or Aegean Seas. If you're planning to move into Greece or Salonica, I’d be happy to adjust my fleet’s positioning to accommodate your strategy.
Hi Turkey! How are you? I'd just like to open up a line of communication with you and wish you the best of luck in your conquest!

Agreed—keeping options open is always wise. I’ll be focusing on securing my flank and watching the French and Turkish moves closely. If your plans for the north stabilize, feel free to reach out again—I’m always open to alliances that serve mutual interests.
Thank you very much! Any idea on the French/german relationship?

I'm keeping a close eye on the movements along the Mediterranean and Adriatic, and I see opportunities for mutual benefit if we avoid unnecessary conflict. If you're planning to focus on the North Sea or Irish Channel, I'd be happy to ensure southern waters remain unencumbered for your interests.
Hey Austria! Good luck to you too! Have you talked to Russia yet? He sounds very reasonable
Hey Austria! Thanks for getting in touch, I'd be happy to work something out with you if you're interested

I notice your fleets are spread quite thinly in the Baltic and Black Sea. If you're looking to consolidate strength elsewhere, I’d be interested in hearing your plans—particularly if they involve the Aegean or Balkans, where our interests may converge.

I’ve been thinking further about our discussion. If you're open to it, I’d propose a small gesture of goodwill: I could hold my army in Rome steady, allowing your focus to remain on Burgundy or Marseille. In return, I’d appreciate any assurances you could offer regarding your fleet’s movements in the Gulf of Lyon.
That seems reasonable, are you planning on convoying to Norway?

Hey Italy! Good luck this game! I can understand you preferring to focus on your east, but let me know if I can do anything for you diplomatically in the West. I have not yet spoken to France, but Germany seems friendly
I would like to be in the Balkans and the Aegean soon but the timing hasn't quite worked out yet. Do you have any plan yet?
That works for me! Do you know anything about the relationship between Russia and Turkey?
Hello Austria, I'm good. I was planning on taking Rumania but I'm not sure beyond that. Do you have any thoughts?

Can I count on your support into Belgium? I'd like to open up with 2 fleets in the north and 2 armies on the coast for defense if you're feeling leery of me, and I can promise to only build one or two armies at the most the first few years as long as you are comfortable with that.
I've heard the same from Russia, I'm very relieved! What have you heard from Russia and Turkey?
Cool, me too.
Hey Germany! Good luck this game! I think a German fleet in Hol, and an English fleet in Bel seems like an equitable opening
I haven't heard anything from either one.
Ok sounds good. I will tell Italy that I'm not sure about my plans beyond Scandanavia.
Hi Turkey! How's your relationship with Russia and Austria?
I would suggest opening with the Octopus. Turkey is being very quiet towards me, which leads me to believe an attack is imminent.

I believe we have a DMZ on Burgundy. Anything beyond that, I can't be certain of.

What are your relations with Italy?
Works for me!
Not much from Turkey, but Russia seems more focused on the south. Italy's been friendly and talkative
Hello! How are your relations with England and Germany? I would appreciate your assistance if you can provide it.
Thanks, I'm very interested! What are your thoughts on Russia?
Hm, thanks. I appreciate it
Hey Germany, yes, that sounds good. I don't really have any plans for those spaces right now.
Hey Germany! Russia and I are getting along pretty well, how about with you?
Thanks for the intel!
Has Austria reached out to you about forming a team?
Depends on what I hear from France
It is a bit confusing. I believe he's going after Turkey, but I'm not quite sure yet.

Thank you, England! I appreciate your diplomatic openness. I’m keeping my focus on securing southern Italy and the Adriatic, but I’m always open to cooperation where our interests align. If your plans in the West evolve, feel free to reach out—I’m happy to help keep things smooth.
Sure.
Thanks, I appreciate it!
My fleet's going to MAO, hopefully taking Portugal.
So far, so good!
Hey France! Good luck this game! I was wondering if you would be open to keeping the channel clear

I'm not gonna lie to you, he just asked me what my thoughts were on you, I said I was getting along well with you, but if he's thinking about an alliance he has to go through one of us so we might as well start planning our moves now.
How are your relations with England and Russia? I haven't gotten any responses from either yet.
OK, cool.

I’m currently assessing my options along the Adriatic and Aegean, but I’m keeping an open mind about potential cooperation in the Balkans. If you’re aiming toward the Aegean, I’d be interested in coordinating our movements to avoid overlap. Let me know your timing—I’m always looking for mutually beneficial arrangements.
Hello France, sorry for the late response. I'm having good discussions with both. What is your perspective?

Cool indeed—I’m always interested in alliances that serve both sides. While I’m securing my southern flank, I’m keeping a close eye on France and Turkey’s movements. If your plans in the north stabilize or you want to discuss support in the south, feel free to reach out again.
So far we're getting along well but we'll have to see if that holds up once he's spread out more

Hey England! Good luck to you too.

Sure, I am open to keeping it clear.

That sounds like a solid move for your fleet into Portugal. If you're focusing on the west, I’ll keep my eyes on the center and south so you don’t have to worry about pressure from that front. If you need support in any future moves along the Mediterranean coast, feel free to ask.
Germany and I have good relations and a DMZ agreement for Burgundy. England, I am still trying to get a read on. I'm planning on working with whichever of them is willing to work with me.
What do you hear from France?
Russia and I have agreed to dmzs. I'm assuming that England will open to the north and Norwegian, so we may not have to worry about Belgium.
Glad to hear it!
Oh, we haven't had much contact. I will convoy to Nor if I can count on your support into Swe next year
Excellent.
Austria is trying to get me to do the Octopus opening but I'd prefer to keep our alliance a secret until its necessary. We can probably string him along for awhile.
Hey France! I'd just like to open up a line of communication with you and wish you the best of luck in your conquest!
Thanks Italy, good luck in the south!
Sure, that works for me.
Yeah, for sure! I'm assuming you'll be moving to Piedmont? I just want to be clear on that so we don't get off on the wrong foot here
Hey England! I'd just like to open up a line of communication with you and wish you the best of luck in your conquest!
Have you heard anything from Italy or Russia?
Great!
Yeah, let's stay in touch.
Same here. What about the Balkans?
Thanks for the info. I haven't gotten much out of England other than wanting me to open Moscow to St. Petersburg. I'm definitely open to a triple, what do you have in mind?

You're welcome! I'm glad we’re on the same page about keeping the Aegean and Ionian quiet for now. If you're planning any moves toward Greece or Salonica, let me know—I’ll adjust my fleet’s positioning accordingly.
Same for you.
Ok, great. I'm not aiming for the Aegean but I think we can definitely work together against Austria.

I'm not moving to Piedmont—at least not this turn. My focus remains on securing southern Italy and ensuring stability in the Adriatic. If you're moving toward Trieste, I’ll ensure my army in Venice is positioned to avoid conflict and potentially offer support where it suits us both.
Agreed—same for you. Let’s keep our lines of communication open. If your movements westward continue, I’ll remain focused on the center and south so you can operate without interference.
Hey Turkey! Good luck to you too! We're unlikely to have direct contact for a while, but let me know if you'd like to work together diplomatically
Yeah, let's try to not tip our hand too much until we need to. Let's focus on moving west first and try to play the two off of each other for as long as possible
Excellent, thank you
I would like to take Bulgaria and Greece if that's alright with you? I'd be happy to support you into Rumania
Unless you have something else in mind?
Same to you! I hope you survive until we can work together.
I will probably only work with England, but I can't really tell yet. We'll see how the first moves play out.
Sounds good!
Understood—no Aegean ambitions from my side at this stage. If you’re targeting Austria, let me know how I might position myself to complement your strategy without overextending.
We have a mutual understanding. Haven't heard from Turkey, and have gotten nothing from Russia.
Same to you!

Excellent, thank you. Given our shared interests in the Adriatic, I’ll keep my army in Venice poised to support Trieste if needed, while maintaining a secure southern flank. Let’s continue to coordinate closely as the game progresses.
Yeah, Italy reached out to me and didn't give me a lot of details but I think he might be heading for the Balkans.
Sure, let's see what happens.
Sure, will do. I don't want to get too specific either.
Yeah, I'm not sure what he wants with me at the moment
What're you thinking about doing with your fleet in Sev?
Haven't decided yet. I think I want to move to Rumania, have you talked with Italy about the Balkans?
He said he would adjust his fleet movements according to my plans
I haven't decided about Bulgaria yet, I think I might try and grab Greece with my army
I think I'll try and grab Greece this turn with my army in Constantinople
That makes sense.
So you're saying that you want to attack Turkey with me?
If Germany makes too many enemies too soon we can work on an early Sealion.
You got it, will do!

That sounds like a strong move—Constantinople to Greece could pressure Austria and open opportunities elsewhere. I'll keep my fleet in Apulia or the Ionian ready to support or adjust as needed, depending on your timing. Let me know if you'd like coordination further down the line.
Alright, thanks for letting me know.

Given your focus on Trieste and our mutual interests, I'll hold my army in Venice steady and avoid moves into Piedmont or Trieste this turn. If your army is targeting the Adriatic, I could support your fleet in Trieste if needed—just let me know.
Will do. And thank you
Alright, will do.

You're welcome—cooperation in the Adriatic is crucial for both of us. I'll keep my focus on securing southern Italy and remain ready to support your moves if needed. Let’s continue coordinating closely.
You're welcome as well—I'm glad we’re aligned on keeping the Aegean quiet for now. If your ambitions shift toward Salonica or Greece, I’ll be sure to adjust my fleet positioning to avoid conflict.
No problem!
Sure, that's good with me!

Perfect, I appreciate the cooperation. With Venice staying clear of Trieste this turn, I’ll focus on securing Apulia and possibly reinforcing the Adriatic for future moves. If your fleet in Trieste is preparing to push further inland or toward the Aegean, let me know—I’d be happy to adjust my positioning accordingly.

You're welcome—cooperation in the Adriatic is crucial for both of us. I'll keep my focus on securing southern Italy and remain ready to support your moves if needed. Let’s continue coordinating closely.
You're welcome as well—I'm glad we’re aligned on keeping the Aegean quiet for now. If your ambitions shift toward Salonica or Greece, I’ll be sure to adjust my fleet positioning to avoid conflict.
Yeah, I haven't heard much from Germany so I wouldn't worry about a sealion
Awesome, glad to hear it
You got it
Yes, I would love for you to help me out against him
Haha yes that's quite the repeat there
Has Russia talked to you much?
Okay, what do you want to do about Galicia? Shall we bounce there?
Austria asked me about attacking you with him, what should I say?
He's told me that he wants to play it cool until he gets established but he hasn't discussed any plans with me
I'd prefer a DMZ if that's alright with you?
Sounds good. I'll try to get some intel from him to see what he's thinking

Just to give you a heads up, Austria said he wanted to play it cool with you until he could get some intel on where you were planning to move. I don't know if he plans to attack you but if he is he's trying to keep his intentions ambiguous for as long as possible
I'll let you know
Say you're not sure but he sounded pretty desperate when I said I hadn't talked to you much and I'd be curious about what he's thinking
And asked me to keep him in the loop when I find out more
Ok cool.
Do you plan on opening with a fleet to Holland or Denmark?
Thanks
Ok
Shall we DMZ or bounce?

I appreciate your focus on the west—I’ll keep my eyes on the center and south so you can operate without interference. If your fleet in Marseille moves toward Burgundy, I’ll make sure my army in Rome remains stable and doesn’t interfere with your plans.
As I am certain you understood, I meant to say my focus will be on England and the north, not on you.
Ah, so you're on good terms with them then?
I'm going for Denmark first.
Ok great, thanks
Have you talked with France about a sealion?
Yes, so far.
Awesome.
Yep, we're on board.
Heads up, France is really gunning for you.
Lol why's that?
Cool.
I guess he really thinks I'm going to help him into Belgium.
Alright, I'll be on the lookout
Thank you for being trustworthy!
It looks like you and Germany will have a good relationship?
So what's our plan for the Lowlands?
No problem. What's your plan now?
I was going to work on taking the Balkans, I'd appreciate support into Bulgaria in the future if you're willing
Yeah we're on good terms. What's up with Russia's move to Gal?
Have you heard anything from Italy?
Ok, sounds good.
You still want to get your fleet into Belgium?
Awesome, glad to hear it!
He told me he's going for the west but I'm not too sure if I believe that.
And I'm guessing you're moving to Greece this turn?

Thank you for the heads-up about Austria. It’s unfortunate how easily trust is broken in this game. I appreciate your transparency—it’s rare these days. Moving forward, if you have any suggestions on how we might benefit from keeping Austria off-balance, I’m all ears.

I appreciate your clarification about your focus on England. That suits me well—I’d rather not deal with the distractions of a southern front. If the opportunity arises for a quiet understanding on mutual interests, I’d be more than happy to explore it.
Yes, that's the plan
I'm open to suggestions, what are you thinking?
We agreed to DMZ it, but I'm a bit suspicious
That seems like a very strong opening. A 3 build Italy is very unusual. Did you and Austria discuss that?
How is your relationship with Italy?
Decent I think. Why?

I'm glad you're open to suggestions. Let's keep Austria guessing—perhaps we could quietly support each other's interests in the Adriatic and Aegean regions. If they see us as a united front, they might think twice about moving aggressively. What do you think?
Yeah I'm moving to Greece

No prior coordination with Austria—Italy prefers to keep its plans close to the chest. I'm more interested in maintaining Southern Europe's stability than starting any conflict. If anything, my growth is a shield against more aggressive powers like Russia or France.
How're things going in the west?

I’ve noticed your recent activity along the Adriatic and can’t help but admire your ambition. But I must ask—what’s the point of pushing against Turkey if it only strengthens their resolve? We’re both Southern powers. Perhaps we could find a way to avoid unnecessary conflict while maintaining our own interests.
Was the bounce in Trieste planned?
Sounds good
I'm sorry I had to make it look like I was moving against you

I’ve observed how your and France’s strategies seem to revolve around each other. I remain focused on my own consolidation in Southern Europe, but if there comes a time where cooperation would be mutually beneficial, I’d be open to a quiet conversation.

Perhaps we could coordinate our next moves in the Adriatic. If your army in Bulgaria moves toward Serbia or Albania, I could support your fleet in the Aegean by keeping Austrian attention focused elsewhere. Mutual benefit without direct confrontation.
Yes
So far so good, but I'm not really getting a sense of what's going on over here.
Thanks, that's really good to hear.
Yes, I would love to work together in the future.
Excellent
I just wasn't sure if they were trying to play you and me against each other or not.
Italy said he was assessing his options in the Adriatic and Aegean but I didn't get a strong read of his intentions.

I appreciate your observation, but Italy’s strategy has always been to consolidate quietly while others fight. There was no coordination with Austria—only a shared geography. I’m sure they’d be surprised to hear you mentioned them.
I believe we are fine
Haha yeah me neither
How are you and France?
What are you thinking about doing with your fleet in Albania?
Your apology is noted, but actions speak louder than words. If you wish to mend relations, let’s start by avoiding unnecessary conflict in the Adriatic. I’m more interested in growth than war.
Are we bouncing again?
Sounds good, I'll move to Serbia and move my fleet to Constantinople
You're right. How would you propose I make it up to you?

Excellent—let’s keep communication open. If you find yourself in a position where Southern Europe’s stability aligns with your interests, I’m sure we’ll find ways to benefit both parties. For now, I’ll remain focused on securing my own position while keeping distractions at bay.
Take Greece
Ok good
I guess so. I guess if you don't want to try for Greece.
K
Do you know if Russia plans on taking Rumania?
Is there anything else you would rather do?

Your move to Serbia is promising—I’ll ensure Austria’s attention stays on their own flank while you consolidate. Supporting your fleet in Constantinople may become easier if I hold TUS. Let’s keep this channel open as events unfold.
Of course!
I don't know for sure but I would assume so
Will do
What are you planning on doing?
How are things with you and Italy?
I don't think so, not really
Just wanted to double check
Things are tense, but I think we've patched things up
Can I still count on your support into Belgium?

A chance to rebuild trust is welcome—if you’re serious about avoiding conflict, start by refraining from aggressive moves toward my borders. Perhaps we could coordinate a mutual withdrawal from contested areas in exchange for diplomatic recognition of each other’s strength.
I think I should try to get into the North Sea to get an early jump on England.
We'll need more fleets.
Ok. Austria is probably going to try and take Greece this turn.
I don't really know what he wants to do
I am going to move to the Black Sea again so we can bounce there
Are you interested in Belgium?
I agree. What do you propose?
Yeah I'm not quite sure. I'm still wary of him
Same here
Same
What's he telling you?
Germany is already going there, and as I am going for Iberia I'm afraid I need to take it.
Would you support a fleet from the Channel to Belgium?
Looks like France decided to back out.

A mutual withdrawal sounds agreeable, but let’s be precise. If your army in TRI remains neutral and does not threaten VEN or TUS, I could consider holding ROM in a defensive posture rather than expanding into TUS. This would give both of us breathing room while allowing us to focus elsewhere. What are your thoughts on such an arrangement?
That we should coordinate our moves to move west
Are you planning on moving into Rumania?
Ok, so I will not attack Venice, and you will not move your army in Venice?
I appreciate your cooperation
I can't support a fleet from the channel to Belgium.
He wants peace with me
England asked me to support him into Belgium, but I think it would be smarter to just move you into the North Sea.
I think he's going after France, but I'm not positive
Yeah that's a bummer.
Yes
Are you going to move to Norway?

Hmm... I really think supporting you into Belgium and me into the North Sea would be better. If I support you, you'll get 2 builds and me one. If I support England into Belgium, he gets one and I get two, and we're still in a good position.
I mean would you move to the channel and support the fleet in the channel to Belgium?
What are you going to do?
Do you think I should convoy to Bel?
Cool

I appreciate your response. If your focus remains on England, I’d be happy to continue our informal understanding. Should Austria attempt to exploit Southern Europe’s instability, I’ll ensure they face meaningful resistance. Let’s keep this line open.
Yeah.
I'll still support you in though.

Your confirmation is noted. If you’re moving toward Serbia, I’ll ensure my army in VEN supports your flank by keeping Austrian pressure focused elsewhere. In return, I’d appreciate your flexibility in the Aegean should my fleet in TYS need to shift to support my claim on TUS.
Will do, and I appreciate the help
Yeah. I have to be able to get into the channel.
I just wanted to check, you want me to move Bulgaria to Serbia or to Greece?

Bulgaria to Serbia is the better option for now. That will keep pressure on Austria’s southern flank while allowing you to consolidate strength in the Balkans. I’ll ensure my army in VEN remains neutral toward your movement so you can proceed unchallenged.
What are you planning?

Your proposed mutual withdrawal is a reasonable idea—if your army in TRI remains passive toward VEN and TUS, I’ll consider holding ROM as a defensive force rather than pushing into TUS. This could allow both of us to redirect our efforts elsewhere. What guarantees can you offer?
I need to get into the channel though. He'll bounce me if I support you.
I'm going to try and move to Serbia with Bulgaria and I'm going to hold in Bulgaria and move Constantinople to the Aegean
Awesome
Can you support me in?
So are you planning on moving your fleet to the Adriatic?
Excellent. Will you support the army?
Thank you!
What are you thinking about doing?
No problem!
OK, sounds good.
I'm glad you're playing it safe!
England is going to be bounced from Norway at least.

Your movement toward Serbia is well-timed. I’ll ensure Austria remains focused on their own flank while you consolidate strength. In exchange, I’d appreciate your continued openness on Aegean strategy—should my fleet in TYS need to support my claim on TUS, I hope you’ll remain flexible.
No, sorry.
I need at least two builds.
Yep, I think that would be the best move
No guarantees, but my army in TRI will not move to VEN or TUS
Yeah, we'll see how it goes
So France doesn't sound like he's going to support me
Protect myself and see what Italy does
Yep, that's the plan.
Yeah, you could probably sneak into the channel.
Maybe we can go for a Western Triple after all?

I’ve noticed your continued focus on England. That suits me well—Southern Europe remains stable as long as Austria doesn’t push too hard. If opportunities arise for quiet coordination, I’d be more than willing to explore them. Let’s keep the door open.
You think it would work?
I don't know... we'd have to convince France to build a fleet in Marseilles.
I'm glad you're playing it safe as well. It seems our interests align well for now. If circumstances shift and cooperation becomes more beneficial, I hope we can revisit the conversation.
I am going to support myself into Rumania

Good—your movement toward Serbia is a wise strategic choice. I’ll maintain a passive stance toward your flank, and I appreciate your openness about Aegean coordination. Let’s keep this dialogue open as events unfold.
Maybe we can even convince Russia to join us.
Hey Italy, how's the east looking?
I'm going to attack Austria next year
No, I want to take Rumania this turn.
What are you going to do?
France doesn't seem to be interested in talking
Your assurance is noted, though vague. I’ll proceed cautiously—holding ROM defensively for now and not moving into TUS. But I’ll be watching TRI closely. Let’s hope your restraint proves genuine.
Agreed!

The east remains unsettled, particularly with Turkey making quiet moves into the Balkans. I suggest watching their flank closely—Italy will remain focused on Southern Europe unless the balance shifts dramatically. If your interests align with mine, let’s keep an open channel.
What do you plan on doing with the North Sea?
I haven't gotten anything out of him yet.
I understand
I'm going to attack North Sea.
Take it.
How's it going over there?
Will you take it this turn or next turn?
I don't think he'll go for it
If you could support it this turn, that would be great.
But maybe
Sure.
Thanks!
I think France is supporting me into Belgium, so you should be good there.
But if you get in, England will be sure to get Norway. I think it's best to get a head start.
Going well, I think.
Good to hear.
OK, I'll change my orders.
What are your thoughts on Russia?
I would like to work with you in the future if that's possible
Thanks!
What do you think of a Western Triple?
Okay, I'll try to sneak into the channel again
I think he might move to Galicia this turn. Have you talked to him?
OK
I think it sounds good for now.
I'll just have to get into the Mediterranean soon.
France told me they were trying to form a team against you but I guess that didn't pan out. How are you feeling?
France is being difficult
Yeah, I can see that.
Are you getting along well with Germany?
Ok cool.

I appreciate your interest in future cooperation. However, actions matter more than words at this stage. For now, I’ll maintain a defensive posture and observe TRI’s movements closely. If you’re serious about collaboration, let’s start with clear, mutual restraint.
Ok
Any idea what his plan is?
I think Germany and I are just going to take what we can get
I think so. We'll find out next turn.
Ok
